2606.13220.title: LLM-as-an-Investigator: Evidence-First Reasoning for Robust Interactive Problem Diagnosis
2606.13220.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as interactive assistants for technical problem solving. However, when users provide incomplete descriptions or plausible but unverified explanations, LLMs may prematurely align with these assumptions and propose solutions before collecting sufficient evidence. We refer to this behavior as user-driven sycophancy: the tendency of an LLM to reinforce a user-provided hypothesis instead of testing alternative explanations. This paper introduces LLM-as-an-Investigator, an evidence-first agentic AI methodology for robust problem diagnosis. The approach is implemented through a Solution Investigator Agent, which estimates the ambiguity of an initial problem description, generates candidate hypotheses, asks targeted clarification questions, and updates hypothesis probabilities after each answer. Rather than producing an immediate response, the agent continues the investigation until the evidence makes one candidate explanation stronger than the alternatives. To evaluate the approach, we build a benchmark from solved technical forum threads in mechanical, electrical, and hydraulic domains. We use a three-agent evaluation pipeline in which a Problem-Solution Extractor Agent converts solved threads into structured cases, a Ground-Truth Evaluator Agent simulates the user while hiding the known solution, and the tested assistant attempts to recover the solution through dialogue. The experiments compare standard assistants, reasoning-oriented LLMs, and the proposed investigator-based model across LLM backbones. In addition to diagnostic accuracy, we analyze how standard assistants follow misleading user hypotheses in diagnostic cases. The results show that the proposed approach identifies the problem more accurately than direct prompting and reasoning-only baselines, while its evidence-first protocol helps reduce user-induced conversational bias.
2606.13220.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被用作技術問題解決的互動助手。<lb/>然而，當用戶提供不完整的描述或合理但未經驗證的解釋時，LLMs 可能會過早地與這些假設對齊，並在收集足夠證據之前提出解決方案。<lb/>我們將這種行為稱為用戶驅動的阿諛奉承：LLM 強化用戶提供的假設而不是測試替代解釋的傾向。<lb/>本文介紹了 LLM-as-an-Investigator，一種以證據為先的主動 AI 方法，用於穩健的問題診斷。<lb/>該方法通過解決方案調查代理實現，該代理評估初始問題描述的模糊性，生成候選假設，提出針對性的澄清問題，並在每次回答後更新假設的概率。<lb/>該代理不會立即產生回應，而是持續進行調查，直到證據使一個候選解釋比其他解釋更強。<lb/>為了評估該方法，我們從機械、電氣和液壓領域的已解決技術論壇主題中建立了一個基準。<lb/>我們使用三代理評估管道，其中問題-解決方案提取代理將已解決的主題轉換為結構化案例，真實評估代理在隱藏已知解決方案的同時模擬用戶，而被測試的助手則通過對話嘗試恢復解決方案。<lb/>實驗比較了標準助手、以推理為導向的 LLM 和所提出的基於調查者的模型，涵蓋了 LLM 的各種骨幹。<lb/>除了診斷準確性外，我們還分析了標準助手在診斷案例中如何追隨誤導性的用戶假設。<lb/>結果顯示，所提出的方法比直接提示和僅基於推理的基準更準確地識別問題，而其以證據為先的協議有助於減少用戶引起的對話偏見。
2606.13220.author: Fabrizio Marozzo et.al.
